name: pnas-nexus-skills
description: Agent skill stack for submitting to PNAS Nexus — the fully open-access, multidisciplinary sibling journal of PNAS, published by Oxford University Press on behalf of the U.S. National Academy of Sciences. Encodes what makes PNAS Nexus distinct from flagship PNAS: gold open access with an article processing charge (APC) and CC BY / CC BY-NC license choice, NO Direct/Contributed (NAS-member) submission track (all editor-assigned peer review), the PNAS-to-PNAS-Nexus transfer/cascade route with carried-over reviews, the broad scope across biological/health/medical, physical sciences & engineering, and social & political sciences, page-based length budgets and article types (Research Report, Brief Report, Perspective, Review, Registered Reports), the required 50-120-word Significance Statement, the <=250-word single-paragraph abstract, in-text Materials and Methods, mandatory data/code deposition with raw-image retention, references format-neutral at submission, submission preflight, and reviewer rebuttals. English-first docs with zh-CN mirror.
